rules.title: Document Title on First Page Matches File Name
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check whether the main document title on the first page semantically matches the uploaded file name.

rules.title: Author Name and Role Are Mentioned
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check whether both author name and role are present.

rules.title: Dates in Revision History Are Valid
rules.verifiable_criteria: Validate all revision dates are valid calendar dates.

rules.title: Version Number Present in Title and Revision History
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check version consistency across title and revision history.

rules.title: Revision Section Is Present
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check if revision history section exists.

rules.title: Footer Contains At Least One of (Doc ID / Page Number / Confidentiality)
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check whether the footer contains one or more of the following: Document ID, Page number, or Confidentiality statement. If at least one is present, consider the rule as passed. Only fail if none of these elements are found.

rules.title: Compliance Section Contains At Least One of (Verification / SOP / Regulatory Alignment Statement)
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check whether the document contains at least one explicit compliance or verification statement where the document declares that it has been reviewed, verified, or is aligned with SOPs, standards, or regulatory requirements. The statement must be declarative (e.g., confirming compliance or verification), not just a reference, identifier, citation, or mention of compliance-related terms. If such a statement is found, return the exact sentence as evidence. Consider the rule as passed only if a clear compliance declaration is present with supporting evidence. Fail if only references or identifiers are found or if no explicit statement can be extracted.

rules.title: Documnet Classification(Report / Plan / Design Specification / Requirement Specification)
rules.verifiable_criteria: Determine whether the document can be classified into at least one of the following categories based on its title, headings, or content: Report, Plan, Design Specification, or Requirement Specification. Consider the rule as passed if a clear classification is identified. Fail only if classification is not possible.
rules.validation_intent: Ensure the document can be clearly identified by its type.

rules.title: Section Contains At Least One of (Valid Content / Description / Explanation After Heading)
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check whether each section heading is followed by meaningful and complete content. The content must not be empty and must not consist only of repeated headings or insufficient text. Consider the rule as passed if valid descriptive or explanatory content is present after the heading. Fail if the section lacks meaningful content or contains only minimal or repetitive information.
rules.validation_intent: Ensure all document sections are properly completed with meaningful and usable content.

rules.title: Document Uses Consistent Terminology (No Alternate Terms for Same Concept)
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check whether consistent terminology is used across the entire document for the same concepts or entities. Different words, phrases, or abbreviations should not be used interchangeably to refer to the same concept (e.g., Employee vs Staff Member, User vs Account Holder, or inconsistent expansion of the same abbreviation). For example, if a section refers to 'Approver', it should not be referred to as 'Author' elsewhere unless they represent different roles. Consider the rule as passed if terminology is used consistently throughout the document. Fail if multiple semantically similar but different terms are used for the same meaning or if the same abbreviation is used with different expansions.
rules.validation_intent: Ensure clarity, consistency, and unambiguous interpretation by maintaining uniform terminology throughout the document.

rules.title: References Exist in Regulatory Database
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check whether all document IDs listed in the reference section exist in the approved regulatory database.
rules.validation_intent: Ensure all referenced documents are valid and approved.

rules.title: Traceability Links Between URS, FRS, and Test Cases Are Present
rules.verifiable_criteria: Check whether the document contains traceability statements linking URS requirements to URD/FRS and/or Test Cases (e.g., 'This system satisfies the following requirements: URS-002').
rules.validation_intent: Ensure end-to-end traceability between requirements, functional specifications, and test cases.
